title: Zanthoxylum thorncroftii
intro: Zanthoxylum thorncroftii, also known as Thorncroft's prickly ash, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and acting as an antioxidant, thanks to its health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Zanthoxylum thorncroftii include coumarins, such as scopoletin and umbelliferone, as well as other compounds like limonene and sesquiterpenes. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is often used to treat a range of ailments, including arthritis, digestive issues, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Zanthoxylum thorncroftii is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. This plant is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. As a member of the Rutaceae family, it is closely related to other citrus and rue species. The Zanthoxylum genus is characterized by its spiny branches and aromatic leaves. This classification places Zanthoxylum thorncroftii within the order Sapindales, which consists of plants with similar characteristics.
